title: Refusal Lives Downstream of Persona in Chat Models
abstract: arXiv:2606.26161v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Linear directions in activation space have been identified for both refusal and persona traits in instruction-tuned chat models, but the two have been studied as separate mechanisms. We show they interact: a compliant persona gates refusal. In Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, we extract a compliant model-persona direction and a refusal direction and intervene on both. Compliant persona steering suppresses refusal -- in Llama, the refusal rate falls from 97% to 2%. Reintroducing the refusal direction partially restores refusal at late layers but not at early ones. Projecting out the persona direction in a late-layer window restores it to baseline; projecting out a random direction does not. Refusal is therefore gated at the late-layer expression stage, downstream of where it is computed. Treating refusal as a single isolated direction misses its dependence on persona.

Title: Refusal Lives Downstream of Persona in Chat Models
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26161v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Linear directions in activation space have been identified for both refusal and persona traits in instruction-tuned chat models, but the two have been studied as separate mechanisms. We show they interact: a compliant persona gates refusal. In Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, we extract a compliant model-persona direction and a refusal direction and intervene on both. Compliant persona steering suppresses refusal -- in Llama, the refusal rate falls from 97% to 2%. Reintroducing the refusal direction partially restores refusal at late layers but not at early ones. Projecting out the persona direction in a late-layer window restores it to baseline; projecting out a random direction does not. Refusal is therefore gated at the late-layer expression stage, downstream of where it is computed. Treating refusal as a single isolated direction misses its dependence on persona.
translated_title: 拒绝行为存在于聊天模型中角色设定的下游
translated_abstract: 在指令微调的聊天模型中，激活空间中的线性方向已被识别用于拒绝行为和角色特质，但这两者此前被作为独立机制研究。我们表明它们存在交互作用：顺从的角色设定会控制拒绝行为。在Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct和Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct中，我们提取了顺从模型角色方向与拒绝方向，并对两者进行干预。顺从角色设定调控会抑制拒绝行为——在Llama中，拒绝率从97%降至2%。重新引入拒绝方向可在后期层部分恢复拒绝行为，但在早期层无效。在后期层窗口中移除角色方向可将其恢复至基线水平；而移除随机方向则无效。因此，拒绝行为在后期层的表达阶段受到控制，位于其计算阶段的下游。将拒绝行为视为单一孤立方向会忽略其对角色设定的依赖性。
tldr: 本文揭示聊天模型中拒绝行为并非独立机制，而是受顺从角色设定的门控控制，位于角色设定的下游。
en_tdlr: This paper reveals that refusal in chat models is not an independent mechanism but is gated by compliant persona, operating downstream of persona.
